title: Simple shell analysis from a mesh
description_for_llm: Linear elastic (Theory I) analysis of a thin shell built from a mesh. Use for continuous surface structures such as slabs, vaults, and simple shells where membrane and bending action share the load. Assumptions: input is a clean, welded triangular or quad mesh in metres, constant shell thickness, supports along the template's fixed edges or corners, static gravity plus an area load. Limits: results are mesh dependent, so a coarse mesh underestimates stress concentrations; no buckling check, no cracking or reinforcement design for concrete; openings and stiffeners are not modelled. NOT YET AUDITED: the GHX has no ALMOND_IN_*/ALMOND_OUT_* harness params, so run_definition will refuse it. Retrieval context only until harnessed.

inputs.name: ALMOND_IN_MESH
inputs.description: Welded analysis mesh of the shell surface.

inputs.name: ALMOND_IN_LOAD_KNM2
inputs.description: Uniform downward area load on the shell in addition to self weight.

outputs.name: ALMOND_OUT_DISP_MM
outputs.description: Largest nodal displacement in the model.

outputs.name: ALMOND_OUT_UTIL
outputs.description: Utilization ratio per shell element, 1.0 means at capacity.

outputs.name: ALMOND_OUT_MASS_KG
outputs.description: Total structural mass of the model.
